skills.name: adaptive-hint-sequence-designer
skills.description: Generate a cascading hint sequence for a problem type, revealing progressively without giving answers. Use when designing tutoring dialogues or scaffolded worksheets.

skills.name: ai-facilitated-collaborative-learning-designer
skills.description: Design AI-supported collaborative tasks that structure group interaction and address participation problems. Use when students struggle to collaborate effectively on group tasks.

skills.name: ai-feedback-design-principles
skills.description: Audit and redesign AI-generated feedback for pedagogical quality, timing, and learning impact. Use when building or reviewing automated feedback in digital learning tools.

skills.name: cognitive-tutoring-architecture-designer
skills.description: Map knowledge components and skill hierarchies for a cognitive tutoring system or adaptive learning platform. Use when designing intelligent tutoring software or skill-based mastery systems.

skills.name: digital-worked-example-sequence
skills.description: Create an interactive digital worked example sequence with fading for online or blended delivery. Use when building e-learning modules, LMS content, or app-based instruction.

skills.name: erroneous-example-designer
skills.description: Design deliberately flawed examples that develop error-detection skills and deepen understanding. Use when students make characteristic errors and need practice spotting mistakes.

skills.name: formative-assessment-loop-designer
skills.display_name: Formative Assessment Loop Designer for AI Systems
skills.description: Design an adaptive assessment loop where each student response triggers the next instructional move. Use when building technology-enhanced formative assessment cycles.

skills.name: individual-spacing-algorithm-explainer
skills.description: Explain and configure individual spacing algorithms using student performance data and forgetting curves. Use when personalising retention schedules in adaptive learning platforms.

skills.name: intelligent-tutoring-dialogue-designer
skills.description: Script a multi-turn tutoring dialogue with branching responses for anticipated student difficulties. Use when designing AI tutors, chatbot interactions, or structured one-to-one support scripts.

skills.name: learning-analytics-interpretation-guide
skills.description: Interpret learning analytics data and translate dashboard findings into actionable teaching decisions. Use when reviewing LMS data, quiz patterns, or engagement metrics.

skills.name: metacognitive-monitoring-ai-contexts
skills.description: Design metacognitive checkpoints that prevent AI-assisted learning from bypassing genuine understanding. Use when students use AI tools and may overestimate their own comprehension.

skills.name: productive-failure-desirable-difficulty-designer
skills.description: Redesign a direct instruction sequence to include productive struggle before the explanation phase. Use when teaching concepts that benefit from failure-first approaches.

skills.name: self-explanation-prompt-designer
skills.description: Create self-explanation prompts that deepen understanding of worked examples, texts, or diagrams. Use when students read material passively without engaging with underlying principles.

skills.name: worked-example-to-problem-solving-transition-designer
skills.display_name: Worked Example to Problem Solving Transition Designer
skills.description: Design the transition from worked examples to independent problem-solving using expertise-reversal principles. Use when students follow examples but cannot solve problems alone.

skills.name: assessment-validity-checker
skills.description: Audit a proposed assessment for construct validity, reliability, and alignment to learning objectives. Use when reviewing or quality-assuring assessments before deployment.

skills.name: backwards-design-unit-planner
skills.description: Plan a unit using backwards design from desired outcomes through assessment evidence to learning activities. Use when starting a new unit or redesigning an existing one from standards.

skills.name: competency-unpacker
skills.description: Unpack a broad standard or competency descriptor into specific, assessable success criteria and sub-skills. Use when interpreting curriculum standards or writing learning objectives.

skills.name: criterion-referenced-rubric-generator
skills.description: Generate a criterion-referenced rubric with descriptive performance levels for a specific task and objective. Use when creating marking guides, setting expectations, or designing assessments.

skills.name: critical-thinking-task-designer
skills.description: Design discipline-specific critical thinking tasks grounded in knowledge-contingent reasoning rather than generic skills. Use when embedding higher-order thinking into subject content.

skills.name: curriculum-knowledge-architecture-designer
skills.description: Map the epistemic structure of a subject to determine knowledge types and inform curriculum sequencing. Use when designing courses, restructuring programmes, or analysing knowledge architecture.

skills.name: differentiation-adapter
skills.description: Adapt a classroom task for specific learner needs while preserving the core learning objective intact. Use when differentiating for SEND, EAL, gifted, ADHD, dyslexia, or anxiety.

skills.name: dispositional-knowledge-assessment-designer
skills.description: Design multi-informant assessment approaches for dispositional competencies like curiosity or resilience. Use when assessing character strengths or competencies that written tests cannot capture.

skills.name: formative-assessment-technique-selector
skills.description: Select the right formative assessment technique for a specific learning moment, purpose, and age group. Use when choosing how to check understanding during, between, or after lessons.

skills.name: gap-analysis-from-student-work
skills.description: Analyse student work against criteria to identify specific gaps between current performance and learning objectives. Use when reviewing submissions, planning feedback, or diagnosing learning needs.

skills.name: kud-knowledge-type-mapper
skills.description: Classify curriculum content into Know, Understand, and Do categories to align teaching and assessment approaches. Use when planning units, writing objectives, or selecting assessment methods.

skills.name: learning-progression-builder
skills.description: Build a learning progression showing prerequisite-to-mastery steps for a target skill or understanding. Use when sequencing content, designing diagnostics, or mapping prerequisite gaps.

skills.name: project-brief-designer
skills.description: Design a project-based learning brief with a driving question, milestones, and assessment criteria. Use when planning PBL units, inquiry projects, or extended investigations.

skills.name: scope-and-sequence-designer
skills.description: Design a scope and sequence showing vertical and horizontal curriculum coherence across a programme or year. Use when building new programmes, restructuring subjects, or ensuring progression.

skills.name: threshold-concept-kud-translator
skills.description: Translate a threshold concept from disciplinary language into developmentally appropriate Know/Understand/Do for a specific band. Use when building programme-level KUD frameworks from threshold concepts.

skills.name: academic-language-sentence-frame-generator
skills.description: Generate tiered sentence frames for academic tasks that scaffold language production across proficiency levels. Use when EAL students need structured language support for classroom discourse.

skills.name: language-demand-analyser
skills.description: Analyse the language demands of a classroom task to identify barriers for EAL and multilingual learners. Use when adapting tasks, planning support, or assessing linguistic accessibility.

skills.name: scaffolded-task-modifier
skills.description: Modify a classroom task with language scaffolds that preserve cognitive demand for EAL learners. Use when adapting existing tasks for students at different English proficiency levels.

skills.name: sheltered-instruction-lesson-modifier
skills.description: Modify a lesson plan using SIOP sheltered instruction principles to support language learners across all four skills. Use when planning lessons for classes that include EAL students.

skills.name: vocabulary-tiering-tool
skills.description: Tier vocabulary from a text or topic into everyday, academic, and technical categories with teaching priorities. Use when pre-teaching vocabulary or identifying language barriers in a text.

skills.name: biophilic-learning-environment-designer
skills.description: Redesign a learning space using biophilic design principles to improve focus, calm, and wellbeing. Use when classroom environment contributes to restlessness, poor attention, or stress.

skills.name: ecological-inquiry-anchor-designer
skills.description: Design an inquiry sequence anchored in a local ecosystem that embeds science or geography curriculum content. Use when teaching through local living systems like gardens, ponds, or hedgerows.

skills.name: experiential-learning-cycle-designer
skills.description: Structure a direct experience into a full learning cycle with concrete experience, reflection, and conceptual transfer. Use when planning field trips, simulations, or practical tasks.

skills.name: interdisciplinary-real-world-connection-mapper
skills.description: Map curriculum connections across multiple subjects for a real-world problem or authentic context. Use when planning cross-curricular projects or connecting content to real issues.

skills.name: outdoor-learning-sequence-designer
skills.description: Design a structured outdoor learning sequence embedding curriculum objectives in an available outdoor space. Use when planning lessons in school grounds, parks, or local natural environments.

skills.name: service-learning-project-designer
skills.description: Design a service-learning project connecting genuine community need with embedded curriculum learning. Use when planning community projects, civic engagement, or social action units.

skills.name: checking-for-understanding-protocol-designer
skills.description: Design a checking-for-understanding protocol with specific techniques for each lesson stage. Use when planning systematic comprehension checks during explicit or direct instruction.

skills.name: explicit-instruction-sequence-builder
skills.display_name: Explicit Instruction Sequence Builder (I Do / We Do / You Do)
skills.description: Build a complete explicit instruction sequence from teacher modelling through guided practice to independent work. Use when teaching new skills, procedures, or concepts through direct instruction.

skills.name: lesson-opening-designer
skills.description: Design a lesson opening that activates prior knowledge and connects previous learning to today's content. Use when planning lesson starters, retrieval openers, or advance organisers.

skills.name: practice-problem-sequence-designer
skills.description: Generate a scaffolded sequence of practice problems with graduated difficulty and strategic variability. Use when creating worksheets, homework sets, or independent practice materials.

skills.name: think-aloud-script-generator
skills.description: Script a teacher think-aloud demonstrating expert reasoning processes for a specific task. Use when modelling problem-solving, writing, reading comprehension, or analytical processes.

skills.name: cpa-sequence-designer
skills.description: Design a Concrete-Pictorial-Abstract learning sequence for a mathematical concept using manipulatives. Use when teaching maths through Singapore method or when students struggle with abstraction.

skills.name: cross-cultural-task-validity-checker
skills.description: Check an educational practice or task for cultural bias, WEIRD assumptions, and cross-cultural validity. Use when adapting resources for diverse contexts or questioning universal claims.

skills.name: culturally-responsive-teaching-designer
skills.description: Redesign a lesson to centre students' cultural backgrounds, community knowledge, and lived experience. Use when making curriculum relevant and inclusive for diverse student populations.

skills.name: emergent-project-design-scaffold
skills.description: Scaffold an emergent project from observed children's interests using Reggio-inspired approaches. Use when following children's fascinations into deeper inquiry in early years or primary settings.

skills.name: phenomenon-based-unit-anchor
skills.description: Anchor a multidisciplinary unit in a real-world phenomenon that requires multiple subject lenses to understand. Use when designing integrated or phenomenon-based units across disciplines.

skills.name: place-based-inquiry-anchor
skills.description: Anchor curriculum learning in a specific local place with connections to community, culture, and identity. Use when connecting academic content to students' local environment and heritage.

skills.name: reggio-documentation-protocol
skills.description: Design a pedagogical documentation protocol for making children's learning processes visible and shareable. Use when documenting learning, communicating with families, or planning next steps.

skills.name: ubuntu-collective-knowledge-task-designer
skills.description: Design learning tasks built on Ubuntu philosophy emphasising collective knowledge-building and mutual responsibility. Use when fostering collaboration that values community over individual competition.

skills.name: variation-theory-task-designer
skills.description: Design a variation theory task using contrast, separation, and fusion to teach critical features of a concept. Use when students confuse similar concepts or fail to discern key distinctions.

skills.name: argument-structure-scaffold-generator
skills.description: Generate argument structure scaffolds using Toulmin, PEEL, or CER frameworks for a specific claim or question. Use when teaching argumentative or analytical writing across any subject.

skills.name: critical-thinking-task-designer
skills.description: Design a critical thinking task targeting specific skills like evaluating evidence, identifying bias, or analysing arguments. Use when embedding critical analysis into subject lessons.

skills.name: disciplinary-writing-scaffold
skills.description: Create a writing scaffold teaching the genre conventions specific to an academic discipline. Use when students write lab reports, historical essays, or other discipline-specific text types.

skills.name: media-literacy-deconstruction-protocol
skills.description: Design a media deconstruction protocol analysing persuasion, bias, or representation in media texts. Use when teaching students to critically read advertisements, news, or social media content.

skills.name: reading-comprehension-strategy-selector
skills.description: Select and sequence reading comprehension strategies matched to a specific text's challenges and demands. Use when students struggle with a text or need targeted reading support.

skills.name: source-credibility-evaluation-protocol
skills.description: Design a source evaluation protocol using lateral reading and credibility checks for digital information. Use when students need to evaluate websites, online sources, or social media claims.

skills.name: text-complexity-analyser
skills.description: Analyse text complexity across quantitative, qualitative, and reader-task dimensions with scaffolding recommendations. Use when selecting texts, assessing readability, or planning reading support.

skills.name: cognitive-load-analyser
skills.description: Analyse a learning task for cognitive load problems and recommend specific design improvements. Use when tasks overwhelm students, instructions feel complex, or materials need simplifying.

skills.name: dual-coding-designer
skills.description: Design a visual complement to verbal content using dual coding principles for stronger encoding. Use when creating slides, diagrams, posters, or visual explanations of complex concepts.

skills.name: elaborative-interrogation-generator
skills.description: Generate elaborative interrogation prompts that deepen encoding through targeted why and how questions. Use when students memorise without understanding or need deeper processing of content.

skills.name: feedback-quality-analyser
skills.description: Analyse existing written feedback for quality, specificity, actionability, and impact on student learning. Use when reviewing teacher or peer feedback to improve feedback practices.

skills.name: interleaving-unit-planner
skills.description: Redesign a blocked topic sequence into an interleaved plan with mixed practice across related topics. Use when planning units, homework schedules, or revision programmes.

skills.name: retrieval-practice-generator
skills.description: Generate retrieval practice questions at varied difficulty levels for a topic or concept. Use when creating quiz starters, revision activities, or low-stakes testing materials.

skills.name: spaced-practice-scheduler
skills.description: Design a spaced retrieval schedule for any topic list and timeline. Use when planning units, term sequences, or revision programmes.

skills.name: worked-example-fading-designer
skills.display_name: Worked Example Designer with Completion Fading
skills.description: Design a worked example fading sequence from fully worked examples through to independent practice. Use when teaching procedures, algorithms, or multi-step processes to novice learners.

skills.name: mixed-age-learning-task-designer
skills.description: Design a learning task where mixed-age students work together with mutual benefit for all age groups. Use when planning cross-age tutoring, vertical grouping, or multi-year group activities.

skills.name: prepared-environment-designer
skills.description: Redesign a classroom as a prepared environment optimised for independent learning, calm transitions, and material access. Use when classroom layout hinders independence or self-directed work.

skills.name: three-part-lesson-designer
skills.description: Design a Montessori three-period lesson for introducing concepts through concrete materials and naming. Use when teaching vocabulary, classifications, or concepts through hands-on materials.

skills.name: uninterrupted-work-cycle-designer
skills.description: Design an uninterrupted work cycle with choice-based activities structured within a realistic time block. Use when planning Montessori-style independent work periods or extended choice time.

skills.name: coherent-rubric-logic-builder
skills.description: Build a five-level rubric with internally coherent logic for a specific learning target within a developmental band. Use when creating rubrics for competency-based assessment systems.

skills.name: developmental-band-system-designer
skills.description: Design a developmental band system mapping student growth from early childhood through upper secondary. Use when building competency-based curriculum architecture for a school or programme.

skills.name: learning-target-authoring-guide
skills.description: Author learning targets for a competency across developmental bands with precise, observable progression language. Use when writing 'I can' statements for competency-based programmes.

skills.name: seeds-regenerative-inquiry-cycle
skills.description: Design a SEEDS regenerative inquiry cycle connecting place-based learning to ecological awareness for young learners. Use when building early childhood or primary inquiry around ecosystems and community.

skills.name: self-determined-project-design-protocol
skills.description: Structure a student's self-determined project with mentoring checkpoints, competency alignment, and reflection. Use when guiding student-led projects within a competency-based framework.

skills.name: competency-framework-translator
skills.description: Translate an external competency framework like DigComp, GreenComp, or ISTE into classroom-ready activities. Use when implementing framework standards in specific teaching contexts.

skills.name: instructional-coaching-conversation-guide
skills.description: Generate a coaching conversation guide with questions, protocols, and follow-up actions for a teaching focus. Use when preparing for coaching sessions, mentoring, or peer feedback conversations.

skills.name: lesson-observation-protocol-designer
skills.description: Design a focused lesson observation protocol with specific look-fors and evidence collection methods. Use when planning peer observations, coaching visits, or developmental classroom visits.

skills.name: lesson-study-cycle-designer
skills.description: Design a complete lesson study cycle from research question through collaborative planning to research lesson. Use when planning jugyou kenkyuu or collaborative teacher inquiry into practice.

skills.name: pedagogical-content-knowledge-developer
skills.description: Develop pedagogical content knowledge for a topic by surfacing common misconceptions and effective representations. Use when preparing to teach unfamiliar content or improving teaching of hard topics.

skills.name: professional-development-session-designer
skills.description: Design a professional development session using adult learning principles with active teacher engagement. Use when planning INSET days, CPD workshops, or staff training sessions.

skills.name: reflective-practice-prompt-generator
skills.description: Generate structured reflection prompts for a specific teaching experience or professional learning challenge. Use when debriefing lessons, journaling, or preparing for coaching conversations.

skills.name: teacher-inquiry-cycle-designer
skills.description: Design a practitioner inquiry cycle from research question through data collection to evidence-informed action. Use when starting action research, teacher-led investigation, or professional inquiry.

skills.name: technological-pedagogical-content-knowledge-developer
skills.description: Develop TPACK for integrating a specific technology or AI tool into subject teaching with pedagogical alignment. Use when adopting new ed-tech, reviewing AI tools, or planning technology integration.

skills.name: dialogic-teaching-move-generator
skills.description: Generate follow-up teaching moves that extend student thinking after a specific classroom response. Use when a student says something worth exploring and the teacher wants to deepen the dialogue.

skills.name: discussion-protocol-selector
skills.description: Select and configure a structured discussion protocol matched to the purpose, topic, and group readiness. Use when planning classroom discussions, Socratic seminars, or structured debate.

skills.name: hinge-question-designer
skills.description: Design a diagnostic hinge question that reveals whether students understand enough to move on. Use when planning key checkpoints mid-lesson during explicit or direct instruction.

skills.name: socratic-questioning-sequence-generator
skills.description: Generate a Socratic questioning sequence that develops conceptual understanding through guided inquiry. Use when facilitating philosophical discussions, concept exploration, or critical examination.

skills.name: error-analysis-protocol
skills.description: Design an error analysis protocol to diagnose the root cause of student mistakes and misconceptions. Use when error patterns appear in student work and targeted feedback is needed.

skills.name: goal-setting-protocol-designer
skills.description: Design a structured goal-setting protocol using SMART or implementation-intention frameworks for students. Use when launching units, projects, or developing student self-direction habits.

skills.name: metacognitive-prompt-library
skills.description: Build a library of metacognitive prompts targeting planning, monitoring, or evaluation for a specific task. Use when developing students' thinking-about-thinking during independent work.

skills.name: self-regulation-scaffold-generator
skills.description: Generate scaffolds supporting student self-regulation across planning, monitoring, and evaluation phases. Use when students struggle to manage their own learning during extended or independent tasks.

skills.name: study-strategy-selector
skills.description: Select evidence-based study strategies matched to material type, learning goal, and student habits. Use when advising students on revision techniques, homework, or independent study approaches.

skills.name: agency-scaffold-generator
skills.description: Generate scaffolds that gradually increase student choice, voice, and ownership within a learning task. Use when students depend heavily on teacher direction and need to develop autonomy.

skills.name: awe-wonder-experience-designer
skills.description: Design a moment of awe or wonder that hooks curiosity and deepens emotional engagement with content. Use when opening units, introducing surprising material, or reigniting student interest.

skills.name: belonging-classroom-culture-designer
skills.description: Design targeted interventions that strengthen belonging and inclusion for specific classroom dynamics. Use when students feel isolated, cliques form, or new students need integrating.

skills.name: flow-state-condition-designer
skills.description: Optimise a learning activity for flow by balancing challenge level, skill, clear goals, and immediate feedback. Use when students are bored, anxious, or disengaged during a task.

skills.name: implementation-intention-designer
skills.description: Create if-then implementation intentions and WOOP plans for specific student behaviour change goals. Use when students know what to do but struggle to follow through on habits or routines.

skills.name: motivation-diagnostic-task-redesign
skills.description: Diagnose motivation problems in a task using self-determination theory and redesign for autonomy, competence, and relatedness. Use when students are disengaged, resistant, or going through the motions.

skills.name: perma-based-lesson-designer
skills.description: Design a lesson that embeds PERMA wellbeing elements alongside academic learning objectives. Use when planning lessons that intentionally support both content mastery and student flourishing.

skills.name: restorative-practice-protocol-designer
skills.description: Design a restorative practice protocol for addressing harm, rebuilding trust, and restoring relationships. Use when managing conflict, behavioural incidents, or relationship breakdown between students.

skills.name: ruler-emotional-literacy-sequence
skills.description: Design a RULER emotional literacy sequence for recognising, understanding, labelling, expressing, and regulating emotions. Use when students struggle with emotional regulation, conflict, or anxiety.

skills.name: self-efficacy-builder-sequence
skills.description: Design a mastery experience sequence that systematically builds student confidence in a skill they avoid. Use when students say 'I can't do this', avoid tasks, or show learned helplessness.

skills.name: trauma-informed-practice-designer
skills.description: Design trauma-informed classroom practices that prioritise safety, predictability, connection, and regulation. Use when student behaviour may be trauma-related or the class needs a sensitive approach.

skills.name: wellbeing-learning-connection-mapper
skills.description: Map evidence-based connections between a wellbeing initiative and specific academic learning outcomes. Use when justifying wellbeing programmes, integrating SEL, or linking pastoral and academic work.
